title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
keyDrivers: Much of this role, gathering data, running surveys, analyzing trends, and writing reports, falls squarely in AI's sweet spot. The work is largely desk-based with no physical component, requires no licensure, and the judgment involved often follows well-established frameworks rather than truly novel thinking. While some analysts will shift toward interpreting AI-generated insights, the overall demand for dedicated market research roles is likely to shrink as AI handles the bulk of the analytical work directly.

taskData.full: Collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, needs, and buying habits to identify potential markets and factors affecting product demand.
Conduct research on consumer opinions and marketing strategies, collaborating with marketing professionals, statisticians, pollsters, and other professionals.
Prepare reports of findings, illustrating data graphically and translating complex findings into written text.
Devise and evaluate methods and procedures for collecting data, such as surveys, opinion polls, or questionnaires, or arrange to obtain existing data.
Measure the effectiveness of marketing, advertising, and communications programs and strategies.
Gather data on competitors and analyze their prices, sales, and method of marketing and distribution.
Seek and provide information to help companies determine their position in the marketplace.
Forecast and track marketing and sales trends, analyzing collected data.
Monitor industry statistics and follow trends in trade literature.
Measure and assess customer and employee satisfaction.

risks.body: Survey design, data collection, and routine consumer insight reports — the bread-and-butter of entry and mid-level marketing analyst work — are now handled end-to-end by AI platforms like Qualtrics AI and Zappi. Lightcast data shows job postings for market research analyst roles declined roughly 15% between 2022 and 2024 as firms consolidate insight functions into smaller teams augmented by AI tools [1]. The WEF 2025 Future of Jobs Report lists data entry clerks and basic analyst roles among the fastest-declining occupations, and marketing analysts sitting at the routine research end of the spectrum are catching the same wave [2].
risks.statLabel: decline in market research analyst job postings, 2022-2024
risks.statSourceTitle: Labor Market Analytics: Marketing and Research Occupations

opportunities.body: The demand for analysts who can translate AI-generated data into strategic recommendations — rather than produce the data themselves — is growing. HubSpot's 2024 State of Marketing report found that 64% of marketers who use AI say it frees them to spend more time on the strategic and creative work that actually drives business outcomes [1]. Practitioners who own the research question, the business context, and the stakeholder relationship are harder to displace than those who own the data pipeline.
opportunities.statLabel: of marketers say AI frees time for strategic work

howToAdapt.alreadyIn: Start by learning one AI-powered research platform end to end. Tools like Qualtrics XM, Remesh, or Discuss.io now handle survey design, data collection, and initial analysis in a fraction of the time it used to take manually. The real value shifts to what happens after the data comes in: synthesizing primary research with cultural trends, behavioral data, and competitive intelligence into recommendations that drive specific business decisions [3]. Over six months, build depth in mixed-methods research design and statistical modeling. The researchers pulling ahead are the ones who can tell a client not just what the data says but what to do about it [1].
howToAdapt.thinkingOf: Entry-level market research is where AI hits hardest. The WEF estimates 53% of this role's tasks are automatable, and most of those are the tasks juniors do first: data collection, basic survey analysis, and report formatting [2]. To stand out, build a portfolio that shows strategic thinking, not just data processing. A certificate in marketing analytics from Google or a CRM platform, combined with a project that demonstrates how research led to a real business decision, signals more than a degree alone [4]. The 90% of researchers already using AI daily means AI fluency is table stakes, not a differentiator. Differentiate on interpretation and storytelling instead [3].

quotes.quote: Rather than solely eliminating jobs, generative AI creates new demand in augmentation-prone roles, suggesting that human-AI collaboration is a key driver.
While embracing new tools and innovation is essential, researchers must continue to strive for balance in methods and audiences, ultimately blending technology with empathy.
quotes.attribution: Ali Henriques, Head of Research Services, Qualtrics

quotes.quote: AI could replace more than 50% of the tasks performed by market research analysts, compared to just 9% for their managerial counterparts.
quotes.attribution: Future of Jobs Report 2025, World Economic Forum

quotes.quote: Organizations most often report revenue increases from AI use in marketing and sales, the field is growing, but the roles inside it are changing fast.
quotes.attribution: The State of AI, McKinsey Global Institute

sources.name: Lightcast
sources.title: Labor Market Analytics: Marketing and Research Occupations

taskIntro: Not all tasks are affected equally. Knowing which ones AI handles well, and which still need a human, is how to focus skill-building.

emergingCareers.title: Consumer Insights Analyst
emergingCareers.description: Translates AI-generated survey and behavioral data into actionable recommendations for product and marketing teams. Works with platforms like Qualtrics XM and Tableau to build dashboards that make findings accessible to non-technical stakeholders.

stat.text: LinkedIn's 2024 Jobs on the Rise report listed Consumer Insights roles among the fastest-growing mid-level positions in the data and analytics track.

emergingCareers.fit: Research design and survey skills carry over directly; the shift is toward AI-assisted synthesis and visual storytelling rather than manual data collection.
steps: Earn Google Data Analytics Certificate
Build a Tableau Public dashboard using open consumer survey data
Learn Qualtrics XM through their free training portal

emergingCareers.title: Market Intelligence Coordinator
emergingCareers.description: Tracks competitor activity, market trends, and customer sentiment by running AI research tools and synthesizing findings into weekly intelligence briefs.

stat.text: The BLS projects 8% employment growth for market research analysts through 2032, faster than average, driven by demand for data-driven business decisions.
stat.sourceTitle: Occupational Outlook Handbook: Market Research Analysts

emergingCareers.fit: Scanning secondary sources and synthesizing trends is the same skill set — the shift is using AI tools to cover more ground faster instead of manual research.
steps: Learn Crayon or Klue competitive intelligence platform (free trials available)
Build a weekly competitor brief template using Notion AI
Take LinkedIn Learning's Competitive Analysis Foundations course

emergingCareers.title: Insights Operations Specialist
emergingCareers.description: Manages the research operations layer — panel recruitment, survey tooling, data cleaning pipelines, and vendor relationships — so analysts can focus on interpretation rather than logistics.

stat.text: Research Ops roles have grown as organizations realized analysts were spending 30–40% of their time on logistics rather than insight generation — a ratio that AI tools are now dramatically changing.

emergingCareers.fit: Familiarity with survey platforms and data quality is directly transferable; the shift is toward building and owning the tooling infrastructure rather than running individual studies.
steps: Earn a SurveyMonkey or Qualtrics admin certification
Learn basic SQL and dbt for data cleaning pipelines
Join the Research Operations Community (free membership) for job board access

careerCluster.title: Budget Analyst
careerCluster.description: Quantitative step up for analysts who want to move into finance functions; requires adding budgeting and variance analysis skills.
careerCluster.fit: Budget modeling and variance analysis provide the quantitative foundation — add forward-looking planning and FP&A tool proficiency.
steps: Earn Anaplan or Workday Adaptive Planning certification
Build a 3-statement financial model as portfolio work
Study financial planning principles through AFP certification preparation

careerCluster.title: Management Analyst
careerCluster.description: Natural progression — broader organizational scope and stronger stakeholder influence built on the same analytical foundation.
careerCluster.fit: Process mapping and stakeholder analysis skills transfer directly — expand scope to organizational strategy and client-facing consulting.
steps: Lead an internal process improvement project and document it as consulting work
Earn a project management certification (PMP or CAPM)
Develop expertise in a specific industry vertical to differentiate as a specialist

careerCluster.title: Compliance Officer
careerCluster.description: Research and regulatory monitoring skills overlap; requires adding legal/regulatory framework knowledge.
careerCluster.fit: Regulatory interpretation and policy analysis skills carry over — add industry-specific compliance framework knowledge.
steps: Earn CAMS (Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist) or CRCM certification
Develop expertise in a regulated industry (banking, healthcare, fintech)
Shadow a compliance audit process at your current organization

careerCluster.title: Financial Analyst
careerCluster.description: Quantitative pivot for market researchers with strong data modeling skills who want to move into investment or corporate finance.
careerCluster.fit: Quantitative modeling and financial analysis skills transfer directly — build Python proficiency and domain specialization.
steps: Complete CFA Level 1 for investment analysis credentials
Learn Python for Finance (Coursera or DataCamp)
Build a financial model portfolio with publicly available data

careerCluster.title: Financial Examiner
careerCluster.description: Long-horizon path for analysts who move into regulatory careers over time.
careerCluster.fit: Compliance and regulatory knowledge directly supports examiner work — pursuing a regulatory agency position formalizes that expertise into legal authority.
steps: Apply for entry-level examiner positions at OCC, FDIC, or state banking departments
Study bank examination fundamentals through OCC public training materials
Build institutional knowledge in financial regulation through CRCM certification
